section_label: Legislature — Structure, Chambers, Oversight, Legislation, Rules

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for a central representative body (a legislature)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: How many chambers or houses the legislature contains (asked only if LEGISL=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=0 chambers; 2=1 chamber; 3=2 chambers; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the first (or only) chamber of the legislature is given the power to legislate
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: How members of the first (or only) chamber are selected. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): LHSELECT_1=Appointed; LHSELECT_2=Elected by Elite Group; LHSELECT_3=Elected by Citizens; LHSELECT_90=Left to non-constitutional law; LHSELECT_96=Other; LHSELECT_97=Unable to Determine; LHSELECT_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether members of the first chamber are elected in the same cohort or staggered cohorts
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Same Cohort; 2=Staggered Cohorts; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Maximum term length for members of the first (or only) chamber of the legislature in years
variables.coding_scheme: Numeric: 1–7 years; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution stipulates a quota for representation of certain groups in the first (or only) chamber
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Which groups the constitution stipulates a quota for in the first (or only) chamber (asked only if LHQUOTA=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): LHQUOTAD_1=Women; LHQUOTAD_2=Ethnic groups (broadly defined: castes, nationalities, linguistic, racial groups); LHQUOTAD_3=Parties; LHQUOTAD_4=Religions; LHQUOTAD_5=Social class or similar; LHQUOTAD_96=Other; LHQUOTAD_97=Unable to Determine; LHQUOTAD_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the Second Chamber of the legislature is given the power to legislate (asked only if HOUSENUM=3)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: How members of the Second Chamber are selected (asked only if HOUSENUM=3). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): UHSELECT_1=Appointed; UHSELECT_2=Elected by Elite Group; UHSELECT_3=Elected by Citizens; UHSELECT_90=Left to non-constitutional law; UHSELECT_96=Other; UHSELECT_97=Unable to Determine; UHSELECT_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Maximum term length for members of the Second Chamber in years (0=not specified; 100=life tenure)
variables.coding_scheme: Open-ended numeric; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution stipulates a quota for representation of certain groups in the Second Chamber
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Which groups the constitution stipulates a quota for in the Second Chamber (asked only if UHQUOTA=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): UHQUOTAD_1=Women; UHQUOTAD_2=Ethnic groups; UHQUOTAD_3=Parties; UHQUOTAD_4=Religions; UHQUOTAD_5=Social class or similar; UHQUOTAD_96=Other; UHQUOTAD_97=Unable to Determine; UHQUOTAD_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Who, if anybody, can dismiss the legislature (asked only if LEGISL=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Head of State; 2=Head of Government; 3=Either Head of State or Head of Government; 4=Government/Cabinet; 5=Legislature itself; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable. Note: if constitution expressly prohibits dissolution, coded Other with comment.

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for immunity for members of the legislature under some conditions
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Absolute Immunity; 2=Limited Immunity; 3=No Immunity; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the legislature has the power to interpellate the executive branch, or whether the executive is required to report its activities to the legislature
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Legislature can call executive to report as it sees fit; 2=Executive must report at regular intervals; 3=Both; 4=Neither; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the legislature has the power to investigate the activities of the executive branch
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Who the constitution specifies can initiate general legislation (asked only if LEGISL=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): LEG_IN_1=Head of State; LEG_IN_2=Head of Government; LEG_IN_3=Government/Cabinet; LEG_IN_4=Political Party; LEG_IN_5=Member of First/only Chamber; LEG_IN_6=Member of Second Chamber; LEG_IN_7=Public; LEG_IN_96=Other; LEG_IN_97=Unable to Determine; LEG_IN_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether a supermajority is needed for passing any legislation (excluding economic legislation or approval powers like impeachment/war)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Who has the power to approve or reject legislation once passed by the legislature (not including constitutional review)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Head of State; 2=Head of Government; 3=Both HoS and HoG; 4=Government/Cabinet; 5=Legislation does not require approval; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Default mode for approval of legislation when no action is taken by the approving body
variables.coding_scheme: 1=If no action taken, bill becomes law (executive must act); 2=If no action taken, bill is rejected; 3=If no action taken, bill returned to legislature for reconsideration; 4=Executive required to either sign/promulgate or return to legislature; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the approving/vetoing actor can veto parts of a bill or only the bill in its entirety
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Can only veto parts (line-item veto); 2=Can only veto the bill in its entirety; 3=Can veto either specific parts or the entire bill; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether vetoes of legislation can be overridden
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Who can override vetoes of legislation (asked only if OVERRIDE=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=First/only chamber of Legislature only; 2=Second Chamber only; 3=Either Chamber; 4=Both Chambers must override; 5=Both chambers voting jointly; 6=The Public; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Proportion of the vote needed to override a veto (asked only if OVERRIDE=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Plurality; 2=Majority; 3=3/5 Majority; 4=2/3 Majority; 5=3/4 Majority; 6=Unspecified supermajority; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for any special legislative processes. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): SPECLEG_1=Organic Law; SPECLEG_2=Budget Bills; SPECLEG_3=Tax Bills; SPECLEG_4=Finance Bills; SPECLEG_5=Spending Bills; SPECLEG_96=Other; SPECLEG_97=Unable to Determine; SPECLEG_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution requires legislators to disclose their earnings and/or assets
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution prescribes that meetings of the legislature are generally held in public
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Public; 2=Private; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other (includes constitutions allowing secret sessions in special circumstances); 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether a record of the deliberations of the legislature is published
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether votes in the legislature are a matter of public record, secret, or both depending on the topic
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Public Record; 2=Secret; 3=Both depending on topic; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether legislative committees are mentioned in the constitution
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Who, if anybody, can convene an extraordinary session of the legislature or extend an ongoing session. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): EXSESS_1=Head of State; EXSESS_2=Head of Government; EXSESS_3=Government/Cabinet; EXSESS_4=First/only Chamber; EXSESS_5=Second Chamber; EXSESS_6=Both Chambers required; EXSESS_96=Other; EXSESS_97=Unable to Determine; EXSESS_98=Not Specified
